comment.body: The story behind Trump‘s tariff game is more complex than meets the eye. What he really needs & wants for companies to relocate to the US is tax cuts. For tax cuts he needs majorities in Congress, which he won‘t get unless he presents an idea how to at least partially finance these cuts. So he proposes absurd tariff plans to all trading partners and waits for their reactions. Whoever gives in, ok, start negotiating. The others will see their goods subject to tariffs. These will give him the edge in funding tax cuts that will in turn be the decisive argument for companies to produce in the US. And it does not stop with companies. With the Western industrialized nations facing severe problems by aging populations, there is a global fight for well-educated talent going on, that can be lured to the US with income tax incentives, as even Trump is aware that the old typical 'manufacturing' (=made-by-hand) job is dead and won't return. Even assembling an airplane is done by robots, for its development engineers are needed. Taxes is the name of the game, not tariffs.

5500 will be the big test this week and i see a few way this will play out. 
1.) Likely just gap over it with volatility this high. From there we'd want to see volatility come down and then this should run up to 5800 (200 MA + big selling block there). 
2.) If we do not gap over it then likely see a test of it. Theres some negative gamma/ negative Vanna there which will act as resistance from dealers. But for this move to continue higher you'd want to start seeing realized volatility coming down (liquidity is coming into the system and providing stability). 

I think depending on how volatility acts around 5500 will be telling as to what the next move will be
